mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns exceed 55% in triggered instances over a 5-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price inflation combined with stagnant federal funds rates forces eventual monetary tightening, creating negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of high-inflation and low-rate observations across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and improving corporate revenues, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are negative in greater than 50% of rising industrial production periods.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing retail sales reflect strong consumer demand and robust corporate earnings expectations, pushing S&P 500 returns higher over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows, or positive returns drop below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in greater than 58% of high-inflation trigger months over a 3-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and equity appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of joint positive-payroll and negative-unemployment observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising new housing starts signal increased construction activity and upstream industrial demand, driving positive short-term equity market momentum.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are negative in greater than 50% of rising housing starts periods.
